summary: Nearly matched Sheik's grounded Up-B travel motion setup function ftSk_SpecialHi_80113838, adding the control-stick magnitude/angle logic, ground-to-air fallback path, motion-state transition, velocity setup, and shared inline setup helper. The PR also matched several small Yoshi SpecialN functions and refined Sheik/Yoshi attribute and motion-var layouts needed by these functions. A reviewer requested using the shared <math_ppc.h> header rather than copying a local sqrtf implementation; the author marked it done.
what_changed: Replaced the placeholder for ftSk_SpecialHi_80113838 with a near-complete C implementation for AS_SheikUpBTravelGround.

Added ftSk SpecialHi setup helper inlineA0, which initializes mv.sk.specialhi.x0 from ftSeakAttributes.x38, consumes jumps, sets collision/accessory flags, calls ftColl_8007B62C, and assigns accessory4_cb to fn_80112ED8.

Implemented Sheik Up-B ground travel input handling: reads left-stick x/y, computes stick magnitude with sqrtf, clamps it to 1.0f, checks against attributes->x40, compares input direction with the floor normal via lbVector_AngleXY, and conditionally enters the travel-ground motion.

In the successful ground-travel path, calls ftCommon_8007D9FC, calculates atan2f from stick input and facing direction, stores input into fp->mv.sk.specialhi.vel, derives ground velocity with attributes->x44 and x48 plus cosf, changes motion state to 0x164, updates animation, pauses animation rate, then runs inlineA0.
In the fallback path, calls ftCommon_8007D60C and ftSk_SpecialHi_80113A30, sending Sheik to the air travel implementation.
Added required includes in ftSk_SpecialHi.c: ft/ftcoll.h, lb/lbrefract.h, and <math_ppc.h>.
Changed ftSeakAttributes.x38 from f32 to int and reshaped ftSeak_MotionVars.specialhi by replacing separate x4/x8 fields with a Vec2 vel.
Implemented ftYs_SpecialN_8012CDB4 as a simple ratio of Yoshi ext_attr fields x44 / x18.
Implemented ftYs_SpecialN2_0_Phys as a wrapper around ft_80084F3C and ftYs_SpecialAirN2_0_Phys as a wrapper around ft_80084EEC.
Expanded ftYoshiAttributes with explicit f32 fields x30, x34, x38, x3c, x40, and x44, reducing the filler array accordingly so ftYs_SpecialN_8012CDB4 can address x44.
smart_moves: Used existing fighter and library helpers rather than open-coding behavior: GET_FIGHTER, ftColl_8007B62C, ftCommon_8007D9FC, ftCommon_8007D60C, lbVector_AngleXY, Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftAnim_8006EBA4, and ftAnim_SetAnimRate.
Modeled related SpecialHi motion variables as a Vec2 vel instead of anonymous scalar slots, which made the Sheik Up-B velocity writes clearer and likely helped the decomp line up with code intent.
Kept the implementation honest by leaving a comment that ftSk_SpecialHi_80113838 was almost but not fully matching and might require more inlining, rather than claiming a complete match.
Resolved math intrinsic handling through the shared <math_ppc.h> include after review, avoiding a copied local sqrtf implementation.
Matched small Yoshi functions opportunistically while adding the attribute fields needed to replace filler-based access.
decomp_lessons: For fighter special-move functions, struct layout improvements can be part of matching: naming motion-var fields as Vec2 vel enabled more natural x/y assignments in Sheik SpecialHi.
When a match requires PPC math behavior, prefer the project-provided <math_ppc.h> over copying intrinsic or wrapper definitions into a C file.
Stick-direction logic often needs both magnitude clamping and geometric checks against collision normals; preserving control flow with negated comparisons may be useful for matching.
Small wrapper physics functions can be matched directly by identifying the existing common physics helper they dispatch to.
Replacing attribute filler with explicit fields should also adjust filler sizes to preserve downstream offsets; this PR did that for ftYoshiAttributes through x44 and x120.
Evidence indicates the Sheik function was nearly matched but not complete; future work should treat ftSk_SpecialHi_80113838 as a useful partial pattern, not necessarily final canonical code.
naming_conventions: Anonymous Sheik SpecialHi motion fields were improved from x4/x8-style placeholders to a semantic Vec2 field named vel.
The local helper was named inlineA0, reflecting a matching/inlining tactic rather than final semantic naming.
New Yoshi attribute fields followed existing offset-style names such as x30, x34, x38, x3c, x40, and x44.
The PR retained established character prefixes and symbol style: ftSk_ for Sheik, ftYs_ for Yoshi, and SpecialHi/SpecialN move names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced a static inline helper for repeated Sheik SpecialHi setup, suggesting the remaining mismatch may be sensitive to inlining boundaries.

Used a dummy stack array `u8 _[32];` inside ftSk_SpecialHi_80113838, likely as a stack-shaping tactic; the PR text only says the function is nearly matched, so the exact effect is not proven by the slice.
Used PPC math-related functions and constants, including sqrtf, atan2f, cosf, M_PI_2, and the reviewed <math_ppc.h> include.
Restructured velocity assignment so fp->mv.sk.specialhi.vel.x and vel.y are written before computing ground velocity.
Preserved branch shape with conditions like `if (!(stick_mag < attributes->x40))` and `if (!(lbVector_AngleXY(...) < (float) M_PI_2))`, which may be matching-oriented.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Don't copy this into the file, include <math_ppc.h> instead
review_feedback.resolution: Author replied Done; the diff excerpt shows <math_ppc.h> included.

follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for a fully matched ftSk_SpecialHi_80113838 and whether inlineA0 was renamed or refactored.
Verify whether ftSeakAttributes.x38 remaining as int was preserved in later struct-layout work.
Search for additional uses of ftSeak_MotionVars.specialhi.vel to confirm the Vec2 layout.
Review later Yoshi SpecialN PRs for cleanup of temporary filler comments and capitalization of x3c versus x3C.
Look for project guidance around <math_ppc.h> and PPC math intrinsic wrappers in other matching PRs.
